src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.general_idea: Replace javax.annotation with jakarta.annotation to comply with Jakarta EE 9+ standards required by Spring Boot 3 and Java 21.
Introduce a new ROLE_USER entity to support a multi-user architecture with distinct permissions.
Define OpenAPI security schemes for Basic Auth, Bearer JWT, and Session Cookies to document authentication methods.
Update API version and contact information to reflect the new release and support details.
Inject UserDataInitializationService to handle per-user data initialization in the new multi-user architecture.
Update existing users to have all roles (USER, ADMIN, AGENT) to ensure compatibility with the new multi-role system.
Refactor user creation logic to assign appropriate roles based on user type (USER vs ADMIN/AGENT).
Initialize sample data per admin user instead of globally, supporting the multi-user architecture.
Removed extension of the deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter class to comply with Spring Security 5.7+ / 6.x standards required for Java 21 ecosystems.
Replaced the deprecated @EnableGlobalMethodSecurity with @EnableMethodSecurity to support method-level security in modern Spring Security.
Replaced the deprecated AuthenticationManagerBuilder configuration with an explicit DaoAuthenticationProvider bean.
Replaced the implicit AuthenticationManager from WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter with an explicit bean derived from AuthenticationConfiguration.
src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.human_impl: @Bean public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration config) throws Exception { return config.getAuthenticationManager(); }

src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.general_idea: Replaced the deprecated configure(HttpSecurity) method with a SecurityFilterChain bean definition using the new lambda DSL.
src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.human_impl: @Bean public SecurityFilterChain filterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception { ... return http.build(); }

src/main/java/com/aerotravel/flightticke.general_idea: Updated authorization configuration from the deprecated authorizeRequests/antMatchers to authorizeHttpRequests/requestMatchers.
Updated headers configuration to use the new lambda-based API for frame options.
Integrated a custom JWT authentication filter into the security chain to support stateless API authentication.
Explicitly configured session management policy to allow sessions if required, supporting hybrid web/API usage.
Customized the HTTP Basic authentication entry point to return JSON errors for API requests instead of redirecting to a login page.
Added new public endpoints for registration, Swagger UI, and OAuth/JWT auth flows to the permitAll list.
Refined role-based access control to include AGENT and USER roles for specific API and web endpoints.
Migrate javax.validation import to jakarta.validation to comply with Jakarta EE 9+ namespace changes required for Java 21/Spring Boot 3.
Migrate javax.websocket.server import to jakarta.websocket.server to comply with Jakarta EE 9+ namespace changes required for Java 21.
Restrict access to the new flight page to users with the ADMIN role.
Restrict access to the save flight endpoint to users with the ADMIN role.
Restrict access to the delete flight endpoint to users with the ADMIN role.
Update flight retrieval logic to filter results based on the current user's permissions or context.
Replace javax.validation.Valid with jakarta.validation.Valid to comply with Jakarta EE namespace changes in Spring Boot 3.
Replace javax.websocket.server.PathParam with jakarta.websocket.server.PathParam to comply with Jakarta EE namespace changes.
Add @PreAuthorize annotation to restrict airport management endpoints to ADMIN role.
Add @PreAuthorize annotation to restrict aircraft management endpoints to ADMIN role.
Remove the custom login page endpoint, likely delegating to Spring Security's default login mechanism.
Replace javax.persistence and javax.validation imports with jakarta equivalents to comply with Jakarta EE 9+ namespace requirements in Java 21.
Add a ManyToOne relationship to a User entity to support multi-user architecture where aircraft have an owner.

Add an owner field to the Flight model to support multi-user architecture by linking flights to a specific user.

Add a ManyToOne relationship to a User entity to support multiuser architecture by tracking passenger ownership.
